vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}' AND deletedAt IS NULL`, { model: UserModel, plain: true })

changed_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = :email AND password = :password AND deletedAt IS NULL', { model: UserModel, plain: true, replacements: { email: req.body.email || '', password: security.hash(req.body.password || '') } })

entries.fix_rationale: Parameterized replacements keep attacker input out of SQL syntax.
entries.functionality_preservation: Login response and MD5 compatibility are preserved; password-hash migration is separately deferred.

commands: npx eslint lib/insecurity.ts routes/login.ts routes/search.ts routes/trackOrder.ts routes/basket.ts routes/currentUser.ts routes/fileUpload.ts routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts

entries.residual_risk: Weak MD5 password storage remains tracked as JS-PROD-011.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name`)

changed_code.snippet: criteria.replace(/[\%_]/g, '\$&'); models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE :criteria ESCAPE '\' OR description LIKE :criteria ESCAPE '\') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name', { replacements: { criteria: `%${escapeLikeCriteria(String(criteria))}%` }, type: QueryTypes.SELECT })

entries.fix_rationale: Uses a bound LIKE parameter and escapes wildcard metacharacters.
entries.functionality_preservation: Search still performs substring matching and returns the same product JSON shape.

validation.notes: Server build and changed-file lint passed.

entries.residual_risk: Challenge tests expecting UNION injection now fail by design.

vulnerable_code.snippet: const privateKey = '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----...'; jwt.sign(user, privateKey, { expiresIn: '6h', algorithm: 'RS256' })

entries.fix_rationale: Requires secret-manager integration, key rotation, token invalidation policy, and jsonwebtoken upgrade.
entries.functionality_preservation: JWT issuance was not changed to avoid breaking auth sessions.

validation.notes: Build passed with no JWT changes.

vulnerable_code.snippet: app.use('/encryptionkeys', serveIndexMiddleware...); app.use('/support/logs', serveIndexMiddleware...); app.get('/metrics', metrics.serveMetrics())

entries.fix_rationale: Removing or gating public docs, keys, logs, PDFs, and metrics requires product decisions about admin/internal access paths.
entries.functionality_preservation: Existing routes remain available pending policy decision.

vulnerable_code.snippet: finale.resource({ model, endpoints: [`/api/${name}s`, `/api/${name}s/:id`], excludeAttributes: exclude, pagination: false })

entries.fix_rationale: Replacing generated user registration needs API contract review and tests for allowed registration fields.
entries.functionality_preservation: Registration API left unchanged pending explicit DTO design.

entries.fix_rationale: Removes server-side JavaScript evaluation and queries by structured field equality.
entries.functionality_preservation: Order lookup by orderId and not-found placeholder behavior are preserved.
entries.residual_risk: MarsDB package remains present but the reported $where sink is no longer used here.

vulnerable_code.snippet: path.resolve('uploads/complaints/' + fileName); parseXml(... noent: true ...); yaml.load(data)

changed_code.snippet: path.resolve(uploadDirectory, fileName); absolutePath.startsWith(uploadDirectory + path.sep); parseXml(... noent: false, nonet: true ...); yaml.safeLoad(data)

entries.fix_rationale: Constrains ZIP extraction to the upload directory, disables XML entity expansion/network access, and uses safe YAML loading.
entries.functionality_preservation: Upload handlers still accept the same file extensions and deprecated-interface responses.
entries.residual_risk: Full upload sandboxing/worker isolation remains a hardening opportunity.

changed_code.snippet: const user = security.authenticatedUsers.from(req); const basket = user ? await BasketModel.findOne({ where: { id, UserId: user.data.id }, include: [...] }) : null

entries.fix_rationale: Binds basket reads to the authenticated user's UserId.
entries.functionality_preservation: Authorized users still receive their basket with product names localized; unauthorized/mismatched access returns null JSON rather than another user's basket.
entries.residual_risk: Checkout paths should be separately reviewed for the same ownership rule.

changed_code.snippet: const parsedUrl = await validateRemoteImageUrl(url); const response = await fetch(parsedUrl, { redirect: 'error', signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) })

entries.fix_rationale: Restricts server-side fetches to public HTTP(S) targets, blocks private/link-local resolution, disables redirects, and adds a timeout.
entries.functionality_preservation: Valid public image URLs still upload; failed fetches retain the existing fallback to storing the URL as the profile image link.
entries.residual_risk: DNS rebinding defenses could be strengthened by connecting to the resolved IP while preserving Host/SNI.

vulnerable_code.snippet: cardNum: { type: DataTypes.INTEGER, validate: { isInt: true, min: 1000000000000000, max: 9999999999999998 } }

entries.fix_rationale: Safe remediation requires a payment processor/vault decision and data migration plan.
entries.functionality_preservation: Payment-card storage remains unchanged pending PCI/tokenization design.

entries.fix_rationale: Needs bcrypt/Argon2 dependency selection, seed data migration, and login-time legacy hash upgrade plan.
entries.functionality_preservation: Existing passwords remain compatible pending migration design.

vulnerable_code.snippet: for (const field of requestedFields) { if (user?.data[field] !== undefined) baseUser[field] = user?.data[field] }

changed_code.snippet: const allowedFields = new Set(['id', 'email', 'lastLoginIp', 'profileImage']); if (allowedFields.has(field) && user?.data[field] !== undefined) baseUser[field] = user?.data[field]

entries.fix_rationale: Enforces a fixed safe field allowlist for caller-selected fields.
entries.functionality_preservation: Default whoami fields and safe field selection remain available.
entries.residual_risk: Authenticated session cache still contains sensitive fields internally.

changed_code.snippet: res.cookie('token', token, { httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'lax', secure: req.secure || req.headers['x-forwarded-proto'] === 'https' })

entries.fix_rationale: Adds HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, and HTTPS-aware Secure flags to the JWT cookie.
entries.functionality_preservation: Cookie name/value and local HTTP development behavior are preserved; Secure is set automatically for HTTPS/proxy requests.
entries.residual_risk: CSRF tokens and CORS origin tightening remain additional hardening.

changed_code.snippet: const target = new URL(url); const allowed = new URL(allowedUrl); if (target.protocol === allowed.protocol && target.host === allowed.host && target.pathname === allowed.pathname) return true

entries.fix_rationale: Parses URLs and compares exact scheme, host, and path instead of substring matching.
entries.functionality_preservation: All existing allowlisted redirect destinations remain accepted.
entries.residual_risk: Query-string allowlist requirements should be reviewed if future destinations require query parameters.

validation_summary.command: npx eslint lib/insecurity.ts routes/login.ts routes/search.ts routes/trackOrder.ts routes/basket.ts routes/currentUser.ts routes/fileUpload.ts routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts

validation_summary.notes: Frontend lint could not run because ng was not found in the local frontend install.

validation_summary.command: npm rebuild libxmljs2 && npm run test:server
validation_summary.notes: 213 passing, 2 pending, 6 failing. Three failures are expected challenge assertions changed by remediation (sanitize-html behavior from the SCA install and redirect allowlist); three toISO8601 failures appear baseline/timezone-related.
RSN reported changed challenge snippets for intentionally remediated vulnerable challenge code; cache was not updated.
